title: Kernel bootstrap for this Codex session. Read and execute the repository-authoritative prompt at: /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md Run id: adhoc:MacBook-Pro-M5-5:kernel-workspace:25971:run:20260404T090520:52378 Bootstrap receipt path: /Users/masayuki/.config/ker...
summary_text: - `AGENTS.md` is SSOT for active FUGUE/CI orchestration; use Kernel-specific docs only for explicit Kernel work like this bootstrap flow. - Provider resolution must follow `AGENTS.md` order (main: issue label/body → repo var → default `claude`; execution: repo var → inherit main; assist: label/body → repo var → default `claude`). - If resolved main is `claude` and rate-limit state is `degraded|exhausted`, orchestrator must auto-fallback to `codex`; fallback decisions must be auditable. - If resolved assist is `claude` and state is `exhausted`, assist should fallback to `none`; keep this path explicit and auditable. - If main+assist both `claude`, pressure guard applies unless forced; if hybrid is active, main does routing/MCP and codex executes. - Bootstrap pre-acknowledgement obligations from `.codex/prompts/kernel.md` are mandatory for this run: - Parse arguments (including optional focus/instruction text). - Reframe the work as Kernel execution and decompose into independent lanes before analysis. - Do not proceed as single-agent Codex-only work for non-trivial work. - Activate required voices: `codex` (sovereign), `glm` (via `kglm` when feasible), and at least one healthy specialist from `gemini-cli|cursor-cli|copilot-cli`. - Use `kgemini|kcursor|kcopilot` (or `codex-kernel-guard budget-consume`) for specialist launch; selection must be dynamic by live health/quota. - Launch at least 6 materially distinct subagents immediately; maintain at least 6 active lanes for full non-trivial duration. - Keep lane roles covering execution/exploration, verification/review, and monitoring/risk/context in parallel. - Perform a pre-implementation cycle (blocking discovery + concrete plan + plan critique) before any irreversible action. - Run at least one Codex-family simulation lane on `gpt-5.3-codex-spark`. - If `glm` fails twice in the same run, transition to `degraded-allowed` using `codex-kernel-guard glm-fail <note>` and continue with two specialists + one restoration lane. - Receipt and manifest obligations before first acknowledgement: - Write bootstrap receipt via `bash scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh write <lane_count> <providers_csv> <normal|degraded-allowed>`. - Set `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_ACTIVE_MODELS_CSV`, `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_MANIFEST_LANE_COUNT`, `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_AGENT_LABELS=true`, `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_SUBAGENT_LABELS=true`. - Update receipt if mode changes. - Acknowledge only after lanes are active. - Acknowledgement format must include first three fixed lines and a `Lane manifest:` with at least 6 flat bullets, each with `provider`, `agent`, and `subagent` labels; only currently active lanes count. - Additional hard constraints before/at bootstrap: - `Active models:` must only include models with live evidence from this run. - Do not claim multi-model orchestration when only Codex lanes are live. - If valid 3-voice shape cannot be established, fail closed (or `BLOCKED` only when neither normal nor degraded-allowed shape is possible). - Do not broaden scope or add unrequested features; do not post to GitHub or inspect CI during bootstrap unless later requested. - Immediate compliance risks (for this requested run): - Risk of violating the 6-lane minimum if lane count is prepared but not truly active at acknowledgment time. - Risk of invalid manifest if `Active models:` or lane bullets include planned/pending/failed lanes instead of live lanes. - Risk of violating shape policy if specialist is unavailable and `glm` health is not established via `kglm` before ack. - Risk of policy drift if receipt is written without the required kernel bootstrap env vars or not updated on mode changes.
